skill.name: ckm-banner-design
skill.description: Create banner designs for social media, display ads, website hero sections, campaign assets, and print formats. The skill guides requirement gathering, art direction, AI image generation, HTML/CSS composition, export, and revision.
skill.summary: Create multi-format banner designs with art direction, AI visuals, layout rules, and export guidance.
skill.author: nextlevelbuilder

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk hits, but the weak-cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from design terminology, dimensions, and markdown text. The confirmed concern is legitimate workflow guidance that asks agents to run local Python and Node helper scripts, browse external design references, and write generated banner assets. No prompt injection, credential access, obfuscation, destructive command, or exfiltration intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Local Helper Script Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run local Python and Node scripts for prompt search, image generation, and screenshot export. This appears aligned with the banner workflow, but it executes local tooling from .claude paths and can write generated files, so users should review those scripts before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are direct evidence of local script execution. The context is a legitimate design and export workflow, not a confirmed malicious payload.

medium_findings.title: External Reference Browsing
medium_findings.description: The workflow asks the agent to use a browser to research Pinterest design references. This is a normal design research step, but it creates external network activity and may expose search terms or campaign context to a third-party site.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly instructs browser navigation to Pinterest. The risk is limited to expected web research and does not show data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Localhost URLs
low_findings.description: The network findings point to localhost URLs used by screenshot export examples. These URLs target a local development server and do not indicate external exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs use localhost and appear inside screenshot export examples. No remote endpoint or secret transfer is present.

low_findings.title: Hidden .claude Path References
low_findings.description: The filesystem findings are references to local skill helper paths under .claude. They are used to call expected companion tools and do not read secrets or hidden configuration files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths point to documented skill scripts, not sensitive hidden files. The remaining risk is the general risk of executing local helper code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Scanner Noise
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptography alerts are false positives caused by design words, file extensions, banner dimensions, and markdown content. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher selection, or password handling was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found banner design text and size tables at the reported locations. There is no crypto-related operation to evaluate.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Scanner Noise
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts map to design reference text, banner sizing guidance, and style names. No command gathers hostnames, users, network interfaces, running processes, or system inventory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain design terms and layout guidance. I did not find evidence of system reconnaissance commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples With Placeholders
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes bash blocks that pass user-shaped prompt and output values to local scripts. This is expected for generation tooling, but users should avoid inserting untrusted shell metacharacters into placeholders.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples are real commands and include placeholders. The markdown does not show dynamic interpolation by the skill itself, so command injection is possible only if copied unsafely.

content.value_statement: Teams need banners that fit each platform without losing brand clarity. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through art direction, layout, AI visuals, export, and iteration.
actual_capabilities: Collects banner requirements for purpose, platform, content, brand, style, and quantity.
Maps common social, ad, web, and print formats to recommended dimensions and aspect ratios.
Suggests art direction styles such as minimalist, bold typography, gradient, photo-based, editorial, and 3D.
Guides AI visual generation for backgrounds, patterns, product scenes, and hero illustrations.
Composes banners with HTML/CSS layout rules, safe zones, contrast guidance, CTAs, and brand context.
Exports finished banner previews to PNG through local screenshot tooling.
limitations: It depends on companion skills and local scripts that may not exist in every environment.
It does not perform full website design, video editing, or final print production.
It cannot guarantee platform specifications remain current without user verification.
AI-generated visuals may need review for brand accuracy, licensing, and text artifacts.

use_cases.title: Launch Social Covers
use_cases.description: Create profile headers and campaign covers sized for Facebook, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, YouTube, and Instagram.

use_cases.title: Produce Display Ad Variants
use_cases.description: Generate multiple banner ad concepts with clear CTAs, safe zones, and export-ready dimensions.

use_cases.title: Design Website Hero Assets
use_cases.description: Develop visual directions and hero banner layouts that align with product positioning and brand rules.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Social Banner
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with one platform and simple copy.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a LinkedIn banner for our product launch. Use this headline: [headline]. Use a clean modern style and include one CTA.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Platform Campaign Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Create several banner sizes from one campaign concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a campaign banner set for [campaign]. Include Twitter/X header, LinkedIn cover, Instagram post, and Google leaderboard sizes.

prompt_templates.title: Brand-Guided Art Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Use brand constraints and compare creative routes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three banner art directions using our brand colors, logo placement rules, and tone. Compare the rationale for each option.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Export Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate visual assets, compose HTML/CSS banners, and export PNG previews.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build three HTML/CSS banner variants for [campaign], generate supporting visuals, export PNG previews, and list file paths and dimensions.

output_examples.input: Create a YouTube channel banner for a finance education brand.
output_examples.output: A 2560 by 1440 channel art concept with central safe-zone messaging, a restrained color palette, one CTA, and export guidance.

output_examples.input: Design three display ad options for a SaaS trial campaign.
output: Minimalist product-led banner with direct trial CTA.
Gradient campaign banner with bold headline hierarchy.
Editorial proof-point banner focused on a customer outcome.

output_examples.input: Create a website hero banner with AI-generated background art.
output_examples.output: A hero composition plan with visual prompt guidance, HTML/CSS overlay structure, safe-zone rules, and PNG export steps.

best_practices: Confirm platform dimensions, safe zones, and brand rules before generating final assets.
Keep each banner focused on one message, one CTA, and a clear visual hierarchy.
Review generated images for text artifacts, licensing concerns, and brand fit before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not use the same crop and text placement across every platform size.
Do not overload ad banners with dense copy or multiple calls to action.
Do not run helper scripts from an unreviewed skill installation.

faq.question: Can this skill create banners for multiple platforms?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for social covers, display ads, website heroes, Instagram formats, YouTube art, and print banners.

faq.question: Does it generate final image files?
faq.answer: It provides a workflow for generating visuals, composing HTML/CSS banners, and exporting PNG files with local screenshot tooling.

faq.question: Can it follow brand guidelines?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for brand context and recommends applying brand rules during composition and review.

faq.question: Does it support print production?
faq.answer: It includes print size and bleed guidance, but it does not replace final prepress checks or printer specifications.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill asks agents to run local helper scripts and browse external design references. Review those actions before use.

faq.question: Can Codex and Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
